name: Restoration America, Inc.
description_short: HUD-approved non-profit housing counseling agency offering free mortgage delinquency, pre-purchase, and reverse mortgage counseling to homeowners in financial distress.
description_long:
Restoration America, Inc. is a 501(c)(3) charitable organization and HUD-approved Housing Counseling Agency dedicated to helping homeowners navigate complex and stressful home financing situations. The organization operates with a mission of "Restoring and Renewing Our Community" and maintains a commitment to financial accessibility by providing counseling services at no cost to clients.

The agency offers four primary service lines: mortgage delinquency counseling for homeowners behind on payments, pre-purchase counseling for prospective homebuyers, reverse mortgage counseling for seniors aged 62 and older seeking to access home equity, and financial capability programs designed to educate clients on sound financial decision-making. All counseling services are HUD-approved, meaning they meet federal standards for housing counseling quality and effectiveness.

Restoration America distinguishes itself through its non-profit structure and stated commitment to operational efficiency—the organization reports that over 80% of donations go directly toward client services rather than administrative overhead. The agency also provides comprehensive homeownership assessments rather than simple loan approvals, indicating a focus on genuine financial readiness evaluation.

The main limitation is that the website provides minimal detail about service scope, geographic availability, or specific outcomes. While the organization appears legitimate and properly credentialed, prospective clients would need to contact directly to understand eligibility requirements, counseling formats (in-person vs. virtual), wait times, and whether services are available in their location.
pros: HUD-approved housing counselor status ensures compliance with federal counseling standards
Free services—no fees charged to clients seeking assistance
501(c)(3) non-profit structure aligns incentives toward client benefit rather than profit extraction
Offers specialized reverse mortgage counseling for seniors 62+, an underserved demographic
Provides comprehensive homeownership readiness assessments, not just loan pre-qualification
Multi-language website support indicates accessibility to diverse communities
Reports >80% of donations fund direct client services, suggesting operational transparency
cons: Website provides no information on geographic service area or whether services are available nationwide, statewide, or local only
No details on counseling format, availability, wait times, or how to schedule appointments
No client testimonials, outcomes data, or success metrics provided on website
Limited information on specific eligibility criteria or what financial situations qualify for assistance
No mention of whether counselors are bilingual despite multilingual website interface
best_for: Homeowners currently delinquent on mortgage payments seeking HUD-approved guidance
First-time homebuyers wanting comprehensive readiness assessment before purchase
Seniors 62+ evaluating reverse mortgage options and HECM program eligibility
Individuals seeking free financial literacy education and capability building
diagnosis: Best for homeowners facing mortgage delinquency, prospective first-time buyers, and seniors exploring reverse mortgages who want HUD-certified counseling at no cost. Primary caveat: the website lacks operational details (geographic service area, scheduling, wait times, specific eligibility criteria), requiring direct contact to confirm availability and fit for your situation.
services: FHA Home Equity Conversion Mortgage program guidance
Community outreach and financial empowerment programs
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 101 N Main St Ste 14, Crystal Lake, IL 60014
meta_description: HUD-approved housing counseling agency offering free mortgage delinquency, pre-purchase, and reverse mortgage counseling services.
